title: Anil parvula
intro: Anil parvula, also known as Small Anil or Small Indian Madder, is a plant species belonging to the Rubiaceae family, traditionally used in Ayurvedic and Unani medicine. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in treating various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. Bioactive constituents of Anil parvula include alkaloids, glycosides, and flavonoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Anil parvula is commonly prepared as a decoction, powder, or paste, and is also used to make herbal teas, tonics, and ointments.
